tests.intent: Accepted commands are immutable lineage-qualified rows with stable sequence, tic, ordinal, canonical body, previous hash, command hash, state hash, and frame hash.
Gameplay events are immutable lineage-qualified rows ordered by tic and ordinal with an independently validated hash chain and no overwrite during restoration.
Every canonical command hash binds sequence, lineage, tic, ordinal, body, and the prior command hash so corruption, omission, or reordering is rejected.
Every canonical event hash binds lineage, tic, stable ordinal, type, value, and the previous event hash so output-history corruption cannot be hidden.
Each replayed command recomputes and checks both canonical logical-state and palette-frame hashes before it advances the trusted replay frontier.
Complete canonical snapshots are persisted at new game and every exact four-tic interval from logical tic state without wall-clock or request-batch dependence.
Saving any valid slot creates a full independently verifiable snapshot at that exact logical tic even when the tic is not a periodic boundary.
A snapshot includes every authoritative state field plus lineage, sequence frontier, command head, event head, schema, state hash, and blob hash in stable order.
Load, rewind, and replay reject a changed snapshot blob, state hash, lineage, frontier, or chain head before mutating current session state.
Arbitrary-tic reconstruction selects the greatest trusted snapshot tic not exceeding the target and applies only the exact remaining command range.
Rewind accepts every complete historical tic, restores its exact state and frame identity, and creates an isolated continuation lineage without erasing history.
Loading a reviewed save restores its exact state and frame hashes and replaying the same later inputs matches uninterrupted continuation at every tic.
Load creates a deterministic distinct continuation lineage and preserves the original lineage's commands, events, snapshots, and replayability byte for byte.
Rewind creates a deterministic distinct continuation lineage whose first appended sequence follows the global immutable frontier without key collision.
Replacing a save slot atomically changes only its pointer and snapshot while all prior append-only history and other save slots remain intact and valid.
Only integer save slots zero through ninety-nine are accepted and missing slots fail closed without changing session or append-only history.
Replay validates an inclusive bounded lineage tic range, begins from the requested reconstructed state, ends exactly at the requested tic, and never leaks later commands.
STEP_REPLAY advances exactly one stored logical tic in lineage command order, reports deterministic completion once, and rejects unknown replay identifiers.
A replay beginning at the canonical new-game snapshot reaches exactly the recorded final logical-state and frame hashes for the complete visible command stream.
Changing any persisted command body, previous hash, command hash, state hash, or frame hash makes reconstruction and step replay fail for the intended continuity error.
Swapping two stored command rows or their sequence and tic positions cannot be normalized away and fails the predecessor and exact-range checks.
Changing or reordering a simultaneous event is detected by stable ordinals and event hash continuity independently of the matching state hash.
New game, uninterrupted play, save/load continuation, rewind continuation, and replay produce identical canonical rows, events, state hashes, and frame hashes for identical lineage inputs.
Snapshots, saves, replay cursors, commands, and events remain scoped to their owning session and lineage and cannot resolve data from another game.
Malformed history, invalid range, corrupted snapshot, continuity mismatch, or payload failure leaves current state, replay cursor, save pointers, and append-only logs unchanged.
The history package exposes non-overloaded save, load, rewind, replay-start, replay-step, and per-tic capture boundaries suitable for the fixed DOOM_API contract.

Production is audited for relational ordered storage and cryptographic validation while rejecting evaluator reads, embedded fixture hashes, dynamic SQL, wall clocks, autonomous transactions, and destructive history DML.
Every isolated history, snapshot, lineage, save, rewind, replay, hash-continuity, ordering, and anti-gaming semantic defect has a focused independently executable witness.
